task_completion.description: The original image is opened in the image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP or another image editor is open with the source image (either a real photo or a synthetic image with 'SCALE ME TO 600x400') visible in the workspace.

task_completion.description: The Scale Image dialog or tool is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Scale Image' dialog box or scaling tool settings panel is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The target width is set to 600 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scaling dialog, the 'Width' input field explicitly shows the value '600' (and the unit is set to pixels/px).

task_completion.description: The target height is set to 400 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scaling dialog, the 'Height' input field explicitly shows the value '400'. The chain icon linking width and height may need to be unlinked to achieve this exact 600x400 combination.

task_completion.description: The scaling operation is executed and reflected on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the main workspace appears resized. The editor's status bar, window title, or rulers indicate the new dimensions are 600x400.

task_completion.description: The scaled image is exported or saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename 'scaled_image.jpg' (or .png), or a new file with this name is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the scaling.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through GIMP menus (e.g., Image -> Scale Image) and interacting with dialog boxes, rather than typing commands.

integrity.description: The agent scaled the actual provided image, preserving its content.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being manipulated and exported visually matches the original source image (either a photo or the synthetic text image), rather than a blank canvas or unrelated picture.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'convert', 'mogrify', or python scripts being executed to resize the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is 800x600 pixels (either a real photo from Unsplash or a synthetic fallback image with the text 'SCALE ME TO 600x400'). The target dimensions are exactly 600x400 pixels. Because the aspect ratio changes from 4:3 to 3:2, the width/height link (chain icon) in the GIMP scale dialog must be unlinked to enter 600 and 400 simultaneously. The final image should be exported as 'scaled_image.jpg' to the user's Desktop.
